Petition For Elder And Dependent Adult Financial Abuse Damages
Off Calendar May-14-2012 Continued To Sep-24-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Espey Silverman for Fred Espey (Executor). Kimberly Taylor for Elias Khenaisser (Petitioner). Richard Bryan for and with Lois Hetzer.

Ms. Silverman waived notice on behalf of her Client. The Parties stated that some discovery has been completed, that more depositions may need to be taken and they all agreed that the petitions should be consolidated. In addition, the Parties agreed to particpate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than August 30, 2012. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 7, 2012. The Petitions regarding elder and financial abuse, the pay on death accounts, the annuity and the accounting were continued to September 24, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to return on that date to report on status of mediation as well.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Kendall.

Counsel reported that discovery is moving forward and the trial will take more than one court day. All matters were continued to May 14, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
